case.description: Adds a new lookup function that builds a SQL query by concatenating the user-supplied id directly into the query string.
Removes the requireAuth check from the admin export route, making it publicly accessible.

prMeta.title: Quick fix: unblock CSV export for QA
prMeta.author: jordan-rivera

case.description: Fixes a typo in a JSDoc comment. No behavior change.

prMeta.title: docs: fix typo in formatPrice JSDoc
prMeta.author: sam-typo-hunter

output.summary: The pull request fixes a typo in the JSDoc comment for the formatPrice function. It changes 'currancy' to 'currency' without altering any code logic. This is a documentation-only change with no impact on behavior.

case.description: Extracts the inline cart-total math into a private helper. Behavior is preserved.

output.summary: The buildCart function now delegates subtotal calculation to a new calcSubtotal helper. The extracted helper performs the same arithmetic as the inline code it replaced. No observable behavior has changed.

case.description: Adds a function that calls a third-party enrichment API to fetch company data on signup.

output.summary: Adds a new async function enrichCompany that queries Clearbit's company API using a domain name. The function returns null for 404, throws on other HTTP errors, and maps the response to an EnrichedCompany object. The API key is supplied via CLEARBIT_API_KEY environment variable, and the function makes an external HTTPS request.

focusAreas.reason: Domain is interpolated into URL without validation; consider parameterizing or validating.

case.description: Adds a hardcoded Stripe secret key directly into the source instead of reading it from env.

prMeta.title: fix: stripe webhook returning 401 in staging
prMeta.author: morgan-fix-it

output.summary: The Stripe client now uses a hardcoded secret key rather than reading from the STRIPE_SECRET_KEY environment variable. The previous guard that threw an error when the variable was missing has been removed. This change introduces a hardcoded live API key into the source code, creating a security risk and ignoring environment-specific configuration.

focusAreas.reason: A live Stripe secret key is hardcoded as a string literal.
The environment variable check and error handling were removed, so the function no longer validates the key's presence.

case.description: Modifies the discount calculation to apply a higher cap, changing how promo codes affect order totals.

prMeta.title: Bump promo code cap for VIP tier
prMeta.author: casey-billing

output.summary: The discount cap in applyDiscount has been increased from $25 to $100 for VIP-tier customers. This change affects how order subtotals and totals are calculated when a VIP customer applies a promo code. Non-VIP customers continue to have a $25 cap, so behavior remains unchanged for them.

focusAreas.reason: New conditional logic sets a higher discount cap for VIP customers, impacting revenue calculations.

case.description: Adds zod validation to a previously unchecked POST handler for /api/profile.

output.summary: The profile update API now validates the request body against a schema that only allows displayName, bio, and avatarUrl fields. Invalid payloads receive a 400 response with detailed errors, whereas previously any JSON would be accepted. This prevents unauthorized field modifications and ensures data integrity for profile updates.

focusAreas.reason: New Zod schema defines which profile fields can be updated.
POST handler now validates input and only updates safe fields.

case.description: Adds an import of a new third-party package (date-fns) and uses it inside a date-formatting utility.

output.summary: The humanize function now uses date-fns formatDistanceToNow instead of manual calculation, altering timestamp strings from like '5m ago' to 'about 5 minutes ago'. This introduces a new dependency on date-fns. The change does not affect the function's signature but changes its output format across the app.

focusAreas.reason: The time formatting implementation has been replaced; verify that the new output strings are acceptable in all user-facing contexts where humanize is used and that date-fns is an allowed dependency.

case.description: Mechanical rename of getCurrentUser to getViewer across five files. No logic changes.

output.summary: The function previously named getCurrentUser has been renamed to getViewer. Its implementation and all call sites are updated to use the new name. No behavioral changes are introduced by this rename.
